name: Live Defect Detection — Automotive
type: Production CV system · live automotive lines
short: Real-time computer vision for defect detection on live automotive and manufacturing lines — running at 97–98% accuracy with near-real-time inference. First production deployment shipped within 6 weeks of onboarding.
problem: On a two-wheeler assembly line, quality issues were caught downstream — costly to rework, slow to detect, and dependent on operator attention. The team needed a vision system that could match a trained inspector, hold up to plant-grade reliability, and not drown supervisors in false positives.
opportunity: defect classification on live camera feeds
object counting for industrial workflows at 99% accuracy
scalable inference APIs that fit existing plant integration
architecture: PyTorch · YOLO · PatchCore models trained per line
Flask + Docker inference APIs running line-side
Operator console with accept / dismiss controls
decisions: Model selection driven by data availability and labelling constraints, not benchmark accuracy.
Inference latency budget set by the production tact-time, not by ML convenience.
Defect taxonomy co-designed with the quality team so every alert mapped to a clear action.
First production deployment scoped to ship in 6 weeks of onboarding — feature cuts negotiated, not avoided.
experience: Line-side defect feed with accept / dismiss controls.
Object-counting view at 99% accuracy for industrial clients.
Supervisor dashboard tracking daily defect mix and trend.
Engineering view for model performance per line and SKU.
impact: 97–98% defect detection accuracy on live automotive lines.
First production deployment shipped within 6 weeks of onboarding.
Inference pipelines hardened for plant-grade reliability and latency.
reflection: 97% on a live line beats 99% in a notebook. The hardest part isn't the model — it's surviving the floor, the lighting, the operators, and the labelling budget you actually have.
